talk_title: Immunotherapy-induced extracellular matrix remodeling and BMP1-targeted ADC
tumor_types: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

models.name: head and neck SCC patients (Roche study)

MOA_summary: Anti-PD-1 therapy activates CD8 T cells that secrete BMP1, which activates LOX and remodels the extracellular matrix, creating a physical and immunosuppressive barrier that limits T cell infiltration; inhibition of BMP1 with a small‑molecule or BMP1‑ADC restores ECM permissiveness and enhances anti‑tumor immunity.
key_findings: Anti‑PD‑1 treatment induces dense collagen I/III deposition in 4T1 and Lewis lung tumors via CD8 T‑cell‑derived BMP1.
SCID mice lacking T cells do not show ECM remodeling after anti‑PD‑1, which is rescued by adoptive transfer of T cells.
BMP1 expression is up‑regulated in CD8 T cells and plasma after anti‑PD‑1 in mice and in head‑and‑neck SCC patients.
Combination of anti‑PD‑1 with the BMP1 inhibitor UK‑383367 restores tumor sensitivity and reduces ECM density.
A BMP1‑ADC (antibody‑drug conjugate delivering the BMP1 inhibitor) retards tumor growth in multiple models.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: BMP1-mediated ECM creates a barrier that limits T‑cell infiltration and contributes to ICI resistance.

combination_strategies.rationale: BMP1 inhibition reverses ECM‑mediated resistance to checkpoint blockade
combination_strategies.phenotype: restored tumor sensitivity and reduced collagen density

open_questions: Does BMP1 expression in CD8 T cells predict response or hyper‑progression to ICI in larger patient cohorts?
What upstream signals drive BMP1 release from activated T cells?

talk_title: Aging‑driven arginase‑1 mediated NK cell suppression promotes metastatic colonization

biomarkers.name: ARG1 expression
biomarkers.finding: higher ARG1 in aged lung/liver correlates with decreased survival in TCGA melanoma

models.name: YUM 1.7 melanoma
models.genotype_or_key_features: C57BL/6 background, young (8‑12 wk) vs aged (12‑16 mo) mice

models.name: melanoma liver colonization
models.genotype_or_key_features: 50k cells injected, young vs aged mice

clinical_data.value: significantly increased in aged lung tissue
increased from 5% to 12% of CD45+ cells

MOA_summary: Aging increases ARG1 expression in lung and liver myeloid cells and fibroblasts, depleting arginine and suppressing NK cell proliferation and cytotoxicity; pharmacologic inhibition of ARG1 restores NK cell numbers and function, reducing age‑associated metastatic outgrowth of melanoma.
key_findings: Aged lung and liver exhibit markedly higher ARG1 expression in CD45+ myeloid cells and fibroblasts.
Recombinant ARG1 administration to young mice reactivates dormant melanoma cells and increases liver colonization.
ARG1 treatment selectively reduces NK cell infiltration and activation in metastatic sites.
ARG1 inhibition with CB‑1158 in aged mice significantly decreases liver metastasis and restores NK cell frequency and activation markers (decreased PD‑1, increased DNAM‑1).
ARG1 high expression in TCGA melanoma patients correlates with poorer overall survival and is enriched in patients >55 years.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: ARG1-mediated arginine depletion selectively impairs NK cell cytotoxicity without affecting primary tumor growth.

combination_strategies.rationale: Counteracts age‑induced arginine depletion to restore NK cell anti‑tumor activity
combination_strategies.phenotype: significant reduction in liver metastasis in aged mice

open_questions: What upstream aging signals drive fibroblast and myeloid ARG1 up‑regulation?
Can ARG1 inhibition synergize with checkpoint blockade in aged hosts?
